perturbation.original_text: Chase reserves the right to change any and all of the terms and conditions in this Agreement, at any time and in its sole discretion, by posting a new agreement on its Website. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the amount of Commissions, the definition of Approved Accounts, and all other provisions of this Agreement are subject to change without notice other than posting such information on the Chase Website. IF ANY MODIFICATION IS UNACCEPTABLE TO AFFILIATE, THE ONLY RECOURSE IS TO TERMINATE THIS AGREEMENT. AFFILIATE'S CONTINUED PARTICIPATION IN THE AFFILIATE PROGRAM FOLLOWING CHASE'S POSTING OF A NEW AGREEMENT ON ITS WEB SITE WILL CONSTITUTE BINDING ACCEPTANCE OF THE CHANGE.
perturbation.changed_text: Chase reserves the right to change any and all of the terms and conditions in this Agreement, at any time and in its sole discretion, by posting a new agreement on its Website. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the amount of Commissions, the definition of Approved Accounts, and all other provisions of this Agreement are subject to change without notice other than posting such information on the Chase Website. AFFILIATE'S CONTINUED PARTICIPATION IN THE AFFILIATE PROGRAM FOLLOWING CHASE'S POSTING OF A NEW AGREEMENT ON ITS WEB SITE WILL CONSTITUTE BINDING ACCEPTANCE OF THE CHANGE.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the sentence "IF ANY MODIFICATION IS UNACCEPTABLE TO AFFILIATE, THE ONLY RECOURSE IS TO TERMINATE THIS AGREEMENT.", the contract effectively eliminates the affiliate's explicit right to terminate the agreement if they disagree with the modified terms. This creates a situation where the affiliate may be forced to accept unfavorable changes or be in breach of contract if they cease participation without explicit termination rights, potentially violating consumer protection laws regarding unfair contract terms.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 15 U.S. Code § 2302 - Rules governing contents of warranties
perturbation.law_explanation: This change contradicts 15 U.S.C. § 2302, which implies clear and conspicuous disclosure of warranty terms. By removing the affiliate's explicit recourse to terminate the agreement upon modifications, the contract obscures the conditions under which the affiliate is bound, potentially creating an unfair or deceptive practice.

Rules governing contents of warranties (a) Full and conspicuous disclosure of terms and conditions; additional requirements for contents In order to improve the adequacy of information available to consumers, prevent deception, and improve competition in the marketing of consumer products, any warrantor warranting a consumer product to a consumer by means of a written warranty shall, to the extent required by rules of the Commission, fully and conspicuou
perturbation.justification: The modified text removes the affiliate's explicit right to terminate the agreement if they disagree with the changes. This contradicts the principle of clear disclosure of terms and conditions, as implied by 15 U.S.C. § 2302, as detailed in Scraped Law Snippet 1: "full and conspicuous disclosure of terms and conditions". The removal of the termination clause obscures the conditions under which the affiliate is bound, potentially leading to an unfair practice where the affiliate is forced to accept modifications without recourse.

perturbation.original_text: AFFILIATE AGREES THAT, IN INTERPRETING THIS AGREEMENT, NO WEIGHT SHALL BE PLACED UPON THE FACT THAT THIS AGREEMENT HAS BEEN DRAFTED BY CHASE, AND IT SHALL NOT ASSERT THAT THIS AGREEMENT IS UNENFORCEABLE OR INVALID ON THE GROUNDS THAT IT IS A CONTRACT OF ADHESION, THAT IT IS UNCONSCIONABLE, OR ANY SIMILAR THEORY.
perturbation.changed_text: AFFILIATE AGREES THAT, IN INTERPRETING THIS AGREEMENT, NO WEIGHT SHALL BE PLACED UPON THE FACT THAT THIS AGREEMENT HAS BEEN DRAFTED BY CHASE.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase ", AND IT SHALL NOT ASSERT THAT THIS AGREEMENT IS UNENFORCEABLE OR INVALID ON THE GROUNDS THAT IT IS A CONTRACT OF ADHESION, THAT IT IS UNCONSCIONABLE, OR ANY SIMILAR THEORY," the affiliate is no longer explicitly prevented from challenging the contract as a contract of adhesion or on grounds of unconscionability. However, it still can be interpreted as an attempt to limit the affiliate's legal rights, which could conflict with principles of good faith and fair dealing implied in contract law.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Uniform Commercial Code § 2-302 - Unconscionable Contract or Clause
perturbation.law_explanation: By limiting the franchisee's ability to challenge the contract's enforceability based on unconscionability (though limited), this attempts to preemptively waive rights that are protected under UCC § 2-302. This section allows courts to refuse to enforce contracts or clauses deemed unconscionable at the time they were made. While the changed text doesn't explicitly forbid legal challenges, it creates the impression that such challenges are disfavored, subtly undermining the protection offered by the UCC.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-302. Unconscionable contract or Clause. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-302. Unconscionable contract or Clause. (1) If the court as a matter of law finds the contract or any clause of the contract to have been unconscionable at the time it was made the court may refuse to enforce the contract, or it may enforce the remainder of the contract without the unconscionable clause, or it may so limit the application of any unconscionable clause as to avoid any unconscionable result. (2) When it is claimed or appears to the court that the contract or any clause thereof may be unconscionable the parties shall be afforded a reasonable opportunity to present evidence as to its commercial setting, purpose and effect to aid the court in making the determination. Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The modified text removes the affiliate's explicit agreement not to challenge the contract's enforceability based on grounds like unconscionability, which arguably undermines the protections offered by UCC § 2-302. Scraped Law Snippet 1, § 2-302, states that courts may refuse to enforce contracts or clauses deemed unconscionable. While the modified text doesn't explicitly forbid such challenges, its omission weakens the implicit protection against unconscionable terms, presenting a notable contradiction. The original text's explicit limitation on the affiliate's rights has been removed, and now there is no prohibition against them making such claims.
